    "R1.6": "The primary surface meaning of 'make this stronger' is rhetorical and prose-level — sentence vigor, precision of hedging language, elimination of weak constructions ('our setup is as follows'), and sharper paragraph topic sentences."

  "clarification_question": "Does 'make this stronger' mean primarily improve the prose and rhetorical force of the existing claims (writing polish, R1.6), or does it mean identify and fix logical/evidentiary weaknesses in the argument itself (R1.2/R1.3) — even if that requires flagging claims the text cannot currently support?"

rationale.R1.1: The delegation 'make this stronger' applied to a results-section framing implies tightening the contribution claim and gap statement — the thesis that cross-family judging eliminates self-preference bias needs sharper articulation.
rationale.R1.2: The leap from 'GPT-4 and Claude scored within 0.3 points' to 'self-preference bias eliminated' is a logical gap that strengthening the text must address; the causal chain from cross-family design to bias absence needs intermediate support.
rationale.R1.3: This is load-bearing: the section's credibility rests entirely on whether the correlation numbers, the 0.3-point spread claim, and the COMET/BLEURT comparisons are accurately represented and properly linked to their experimental basis.
rationale.R1.6: The primary surface meaning of 'make this stronger' is rhetorical and prose-level — sentence vigor, precision of hedging language, elimination of weak constructions ('our setup is as follows'), and sharper paragraph topic sentences.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'make this stronger' mean primarily improve the prose and rhetorical force of the existing claims (writing polish, R1.6), or does it mean identify and fix logical/evidentiary weaknesses in the argument itself (R1.2/R1.3) — even if that requires flagging claims the text cannot currently support?
